title: Practice Problem Generator
text:
Hypothesis Testing with z-scores
Given the following data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly different from the population mean: \(\mu = {11}\)? Use a \({2}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.01}\)





A




18


15


19


14


14


18


13


14


18



The necessary summary statistics for these data \[M_A = {15.89}\] \[{\sigma} = {2}\] \[n = {9}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu = 11\] \[H_1: \mu \ne 11\] 
The decision criteria:  \(z_{crit} = \pm{2.58}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}\) Calculate the standard error  \[\sigma_M = \frac{\sigma}{\sqrt{N}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{2}{\sqrt{9}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{2}{3.0}\]  \[\sigma_M = {0.67}\]  Calculate \(z_{obt}\)  \[z_{obt} = {\frac{M - \mu}{\sigma_M}}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{15.89 - 11}{0.67}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{4.89}{0.67}\]  \[z_{obt} = {7.3}\]  Calculate Cohen’s d for effect size  \[d = \frac{M - \mu}{\sigma}\]  \[d = \frac{15.89 - 11}{2}\]  \[d = \frac{4.89}{2}\]  \[d = {2.45}\]  The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  z = 7.3, p < 0.01, d = 2.45

title: Practice Problem Generator
text:
One-Sample t-test
Given the following data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly greater than \({40}\)? Use a \({1}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) 





A




42


48


40


36


51


32


45


42


37


40



 The necessary summary statistics for these data \[M_A = {41.3}\] \[s^2 = {32.23}\] \[n = {10}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu \leq 40\] \[H_1: \mu \gt 40\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = +{1.83}, \alpha_{one-tailed} = {0.05}, df = {9}\) Calculate the standard error  \[s_M = \sqrt{\frac{s^2}{n}}\]  \[s_M = \sqrt{\frac{32.23}{10}}\]  \[s_M = \sqrt{3.22}\]  \[s_M = {1.79}\]  calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{M - \mu}{s_M}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{41.3 - 40}{1.79}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{1.3}{1.79}\]  \[t_{obt} = {0.73}\]  calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M - \mu}{s}\]  \[d = \frac{41.3 - 40}{5.68}\]  \[d = \frac{1.3}{5.68}\]  \[d = {0.23}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(9) = 0.73, p > 0.05, d = 0.23

title: Practice Problem Generator
text:
Independent-Samples t-test
Given the following between-subjects data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly different from the mean of \(Group_B\)? Use a \({2}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.05}\)





A
B




36
42


45

62

57
60

53

38

 The necessary summary Statistis for these data: \[M_A = {45.4}, M_B = {55.2}\] \[SS_A = {1369.6}, SS_B = {1066.4}\] \[n_A = {15}, n_B = {15}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B = 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \ne 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{2.05}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.05}, df = {28}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{1369.6 + 1066.4}{14 + 14}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{2436.0}{28}\]  \[s_p^2 = {87.0}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{87.0}{15} + \frac{87.0}{15}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{5.8 + 5.8}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{11.6}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {3.41}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(45.4 - 55.2) - {0}}{3.41}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-9.8}{3.41}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-2.87}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(45.4 - 55.2)}{{{\sqrt{87.0}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(-9.8)}{9.33}\]  \[d = {-1.05}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(28) = -2.87, p < 0.05, d = -1.05

New Summary statistics for the difference scores: \[M_D = {-3.56}, \quad \Sigma{D} = {-32}, \quad \Sigma{D^2} = {196}\] 
Calculate SS of the difference scores  \[SS_D = \Sigma D^2 - \frac{(\Sigma D)^2}{n}\]  \[SS_D = {196} - \frac{1024}{9}\]  \[SS_D = {196} - {113.78}\]  \[SS_D = {82.22}\] 
Calculate the variance of the difference scores  \[s_D^2 = \frac{SS_D}{df}\]  \[s_D^2 = \frac{82.22}{8}\]  \[s_D^2 = {10.28}\] 
Calculate the estimated standard error of the difference scores  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{s^2}{n}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{10.28}{9}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{1.14}\]  \[s_{M_D} = {1.07}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{M_D - \mu_D}{s_{M_D}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-3.56 - 0}{1.07}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-3.56}{1.07}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-3.33}\] 
Calculating Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_D}{\sqrt{s^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{-3.56}{{{\sqrt{10.28}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{-3.56}{3.21}\]  \[d = {-1.11}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(8) = -3.33, p > 0.01, d = -1.11

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {2534} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {148818} \quad k = {3} \quad N = {45}\) 
\(T_{A} = {858} \quad SS_{A} = {1668.4}\)  \(T_{B} = {764} \quad SS_{B} = {1388.93}\)  \(T_{C} = {912} \quad SS_{C} = {2320.4}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.22}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {45} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {44}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {2}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {45} - {3}\]  \[df_{within} = {42}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {148818} - \frac{2534^2}{45}\]  \[SS_{total} = {148818} - \frac{6421156}{45}\]  \[SS_{total} = {148818} - {142692.36}\]  \[SS_{total} = {6125.64}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {1668.4 + 1388.93 + 2320.4}\]  \[SS_{within} = {5377.73}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {6125.64} - {5377.73}\]  \[SS_{between} = {747.91}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{747.91}{2}\]  \[MS_{between} = {373.96}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{5377.73}{42}\]  \[MS_{within} = {128.04}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{373.96}{128.04}\]  \[F_{obt} = {2.92}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{747.91}{6125.64}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.12}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {42}) = {2.92}, p > {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.12}\)

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {1331} \quad \Sigma X^2 = {48501} \quad k = {5} \quad N = {40}\) 
\(T_{A} = {294} \quad SS_{A} = {769.5}\)  \(T_{B} = {211} \quad SS_{B} = {259.88}\)  \(T_{C} = {347} \quad SS_{C} = {605.88}\)  \(T_{D} = {280} \quad SS_{D} = {382.0}\)  \(T_{E} = {199} \quad SS_{E} = {312.88}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D = \mu_E\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.07}, \alpha = {0.01}\) 
Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {40} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {39}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {5} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {40} - {5}\]  \[df_{within} = {35}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {48501} - \frac{1331^2}{40}\]  \[SS_{total} = {48501} - \frac{1771561}{40}\]  \[SS_{total} = {48501} - {44289.02}\]  \[SS_{total} = {4211.98}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {769.5 + 259.88 + 605.88 + 382.0 + 312.88}\]  \[SS_{within} = {2330.14}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {4211.98} - {2330.14}\]  \[SS_{between} = {1881.84}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {8} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {7}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {35} - {7}\]  \[df_{error} = {28}\] 
Calculate person sums and add a new column (P)

\[SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{184^2}{5} + \frac{151^2}{5} + \frac{171^2}{5} + \frac{160^2}{5} + \frac{165^2}{5} + \frac{176^2}{5} + \frac{188^2}{5} + \frac{136^2}{5}} - \frac{1331^2}{40}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{33856}{5} + \frac{22801}{5} + \frac{29241}{5} + \frac{25600}{5} + \frac{27225}{5} + \frac{30976}{5} + \frac{35344}{5} + \frac{18496}{5}} - \frac{1771561}{40}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {{6771.2} + {4560.2} + {5848.2} + {5120.0} + {5445.0} + {6195.2} + {7068.8} + {3699.2}} - {44289.02}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {44707.8} - {44289.02}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {418.78}\]  \[SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}\]  \[SS_{error} = {2330.14} - {418.78}\]  \[SS_{error} = {1911.36}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{1881.84}{4}\]  \[MS_{between} = {470.46}\] 
\[MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}\]  \[MS_{error} = \frac{1911.36}{28}\]  \[MS_{error} = {68.26}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{470.46}{68.26}\]  \[F_{obt} = {6.89}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{1881.84}{{4211.98} - {418.78}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{1881.84}{3793.2}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.5}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {28}) = {6.89}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.5}\)

title: Practice Problem Generator
text:
Two-Factor ANOVA
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {782} \quad M_{A1B1} = {52.13}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {2833.73} \quad n_{A1B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {858} \quad M_{A1B2} = {57.2}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {2410.4} \quad n_{A1B2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {1029} \quad M_{A2B1} = {68.6}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {2907.6} \quad n_{A2B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {699} \quad M_{A2B2} = {46.6}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {2725.6} \quad n_{A2B2} = {15}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {60} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {59}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {60} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {56}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {203886} - \frac{3368^2}{60}\)  \(SS_{total} = {203886} - \frac{11343424}{60}\)  \(SS_{total} = {203886} - {189057.07}\)  \(SS_{total} = {14828.93}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {2833.73 + 2410.4 + 2907.6 + 2725.6}\)  \(SS_{within} = {10877.33}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {14828.93} - {10877.33}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3951.6}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1640}  \quad n_{A_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1728}  \quad n_{A_2} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.01}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1640}^2}{30} + \frac{{1728}^2}{30}} - \frac{{3368}^2}{60}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{2689600}}{30} + \frac{{2985984}}{30}} - \frac{{11343424}}{60}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{89653.33} + {99532.8}} - {189057.07}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {189186.13} - {189057.07}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {129.06}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{129.06}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {129.06}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{129.06}{194.24}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.66}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{129.06}{{14828.93} - {1075.26} - {2747.28}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{129.06}{11006.39}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {56}) = {0.66}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1811}  \quad n_{B_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1557}  \quad n_{B_2} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.01}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1811}^2}{30} + \frac{{1557}^2}{30}} - \frac{{3368}^2}{60}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{3279721}}{30} + \frac{{2424249}}{30}} - \frac{{11343424}}{60}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{109324.03} + {80808.3}} - {189057.07}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {190132.33} - {189057.07}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {1075.26}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{1075.26}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {1075.26}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{1075.26}{194.24}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {5.54}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1075.26}{{14828.93} - {129.06} - {2747.28}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1075.26}{11952.59}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.09}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {56}) = {5.54}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.09}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.01}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {3951.6} - {129.06} - {1075.26}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {2747.28}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{2747.28}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {2747.28}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{2747.28}{194.24}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {14.14}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2747.28}{{14828.93} - {129.06} - {1075.26}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2747.28}{13624.61}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.2}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {56}) = {14.14}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.2}\)
